title: Chapter 107 – Divine Technique
content:
The warriors spoke with one voice.

Their call of "Grandmaster" to Lightning Genius shook the night air with gentle reverence.

[TL Note- A lot of people are confused about the title Grandmaster. Its different from normal master-disciple relationship. Grandmaster is a high ranking title who studies/creates/teaches martial arts or an expert in martial arts verses, the person doesn't have to be strong necessarily, think of it like a scholar but in martial arts.]

Every voice, rooted in well-cultivated internal martial arts, carried an unmistakable solidity.

'What.'

An impossible event had occurred. That was Zhu Lianting's immediate thought.

This was no ordinary matter.

Even white martial clothes were rare. Across the entire murim, that held true.

Desolate Fortress's white robes were proof of exceptional martial arts.

Indeed, formal warriors of the main fortress were beings who could serve as the backbone of any martial sect.

For them to gather together in support of one person? The implications were staggering.

"Now."

Thorns rose in Zhu Lianting's voice.

"What do you think you're doing? Do you dare violate the main fortress's laws before the Desolate Ma family?"

"How does Lady Zhu intend to mislead our hearts with that reasoning?"

Crimson Day Sword of the Annihilation Team stepped forward, his voice thick with conviction. His blue robe gave him the bearing of a military officer.

Though shabby, as if he hadn't bothered with maintenance, it looked formidably solid, saturated with qi.

"Mislead? What nonsense are you speaking to this lady...?"

Zhu Lianting's eyes grew fierce. But Crimson Day Sword paid her no mind.

"I understand why you mention laws. You mean the main fortress's guidelines that forbid master-disciple bonds as taboo? Truly amusing words. Not the law that's amusing -- Lady Zhu herself."

"Continue. Let's hear your sophistry."

She snorted mockingly. Inwardly, she felt nothing of the sort.

She had intended to make Lightning Genius Jeong Yeon-shin into Ma Se-in's sword.

She had already prepared considerable wealth, weapons, and even rare spirit medicines.

Though a branch member, Zhu Lianting was of royal blood. She knew the type well. Jeong Yeon-shin was not one to break oaths.

If she could secure a vow of loyalty, he would clearly become a precious blade to wield for years to come.

'Radiant Demon Wing annihilated. Just when the moment had come to embrace Lightning Genius.'

She had been silently cursing her mistake -- approaching him with conditions, like a negotiation.

Even so, she believed another chance remained.

Besides the transcendent Desolate Fortress Lord, the maternal Ma family, and the Radiant Demon Wing whose fate had dimmed in Sichuan, what did Lightning Genius have left?

He was a sixteen-year-old boy who had entered the main fortress alone.

Eventually, he would have no choice but to rely on her side. Then she would simply embrace him gently.

So completely that he could never escape her warmth and authority.

That was Zhu Lianting's calculation. She wasn't wrong.

Until Crimson Day Sword brought warriors to pay respects to Lightning Genius.

'A man who crawled along as a white-rank suddenly became blue.'

She glared at Crimson Day Sword's scarred face while taking in Jeong Yeon-shin in her peripheral vision.

She kept her glance hidden. But it was meaningless.

The boy's face, glimpsed from the corner of her eye, was colored with unprecedented emotion.

Even that was subtle, but interest and surprise were unmistakable.

The reason was obvious. Zhu Lianting was herself a martial arts master.

She recognized in the warrior group now confronting her a temperament strikingly similar to Lightning Genius.

They were of one legacy. Bonds had already formed.

'The variable is martial arts.'

It was the same method she had used to shake the Desolate Ma family. But two things were fundamentally different.

Lightning Genius had not orchestrated this situation.

Furthermore, he had begun receiving respect as Grandmaster by creating martial arts himself. Right here and now, in this very place.

"...Lady Zhu, are you listening?"

Crimson Day Sword stared blankly at Zhu Lianting.

She snapped back to attention. Nothing went as intended when Lightning Genius was involved.

Though only the second time, it left a powerful impression.

"I'm listening."

Zhu Lianting raised her chin with practiced elegance. At the very least, her bearing had to maintain its dignity.

Whatever was happening in her mind, however the situation had shifted.

"You said it wasn't a greeting to a master? Hard to believe. The respect you showed to an absurdly young blue-rank was excessive. Can you explain with anything other than a master-disciple bond? Unless you intend to directly challenge the main fortress's taboo."

She spoke in a voice sharp enough to cut. Crimson Day Sword remained unperturbed.

Zhu Lianting held no official position at the main fortress. She wasn't even the Desolate Ma family's formal mistress.

A figure far removed from Desolate Fortress's chain of command. Crimson Day Sword understood this precisely.

Meanwhile, he himself had worn blue robes only after decades as a white-rank warrior.

It was thanks to cultivating Lightning Genius's recently released Radiant Wing Step.

Radiant Wing Step's second key principle matched Crimson Day Sword perfectly. The footwork formula for creating intervals resonated with his entire life of combat experience.

Lady Donkey Roll. He had never hesitated to throw his body to the ground during duels.

It meant he was naturally proficient at measuring distance in the heat of battle.

Radiant Wing Second Step.

He had glimpsed a heaven-given realm in that formula for creating intervals.

Crimson Day Sword trembled at the stepping method that wholly supplemented his lacking talent.

It was divine technique. At least for him. It was more than enough to pass the blue-rank advancement examination he had failed over dozens of attempts.

Therefore.

'Lightning Genius is both my benefactor and truly my Grandmaster.'

Every warrior gathered here thought the same.

***

Crimson Day Sword stood his ground. This was a discussion of Desolate Fortress's laws.

His accumulated years and rank were more than sufficient to speak.

He parted his lips.

"Lady Zhu is of royal blood. The whole world knows the Ming imperial court keeps close ties with Buddhist sects. Isn't that why Shaolin stands particularly strong among the Nine Great Sects? All the more so when even the Little Divine Monk is of noble birth."

"Don't stray from the point."

Even Zhu Lianting's cold voice lacked its usual weight. Crimson Day Sword continued without hesitation.

"I know royals have a tradition of climbing Mount Song as incense offering guests. You must have gone at least once."

"And?"

"Isn't the pagoda forest enshrining Shaolin's high monks' relics the temple's most famous sight? I know that no member of the royal family fails to press their palms together before the nirvana-attained Bodhidharma and Venerable Huike. When making offerings."

[TL Note- Venerable Huike or Dazu Huike was the successor of Bodhidharma.]

Zhu Lianting's eyes widened.

"You now..."

"It's the same principle with Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius. No -- the only small difference is that our martial legacy's Grandmaster is still alive and young. We are like Shaolin's incense offering guests. We paid proper respects to our legacy's Grandmaster. What main fortress warrior would take offense at that? Unless their mind is strangely twisted."

He returned her earlier mocking tone with precise mimicry.

Perhaps because it was humiliation she had never experienced.

Her eyes showed white for an instant as she lost in logic and was refuted in words. Her pupils shifted restlessly.

Lightning Genius now had nothing he needed to beg for.

Those bound to the martial legacy he had created more than made up for any shortfall in forces.

Clear facts struck Zhu Lianting's mind like a thunderbolt. Only Ma Se-in beside her fidgeted uneasily.

"These ones truly..."

Crimson Day Sword shifted his gaze from her rising fury.

He looked toward Ma Yeon-jeok, the purple supreme master who had been silently watching them throughout.

"The Ma Family Head Elder has truly excellent blood relations.

Something those of the Lightning Genius legacy will always be grateful for."

While paying compliment, he also left room for the other side to retreat gracefully. It was skillful speech.

Jeong Yeon-shin could even imagine himself delivering honeyed words to the Desolate Fortress Lord.

"...Your words are not wrong. Yeon-shin has gained strong allies."

"Family Head!"

Ma Yeon-jeok's composure as he disregarded Zhu Lianting's cry was surprisingly calm.

He only opened his mouth while gazing at his grandson, who was building his own name before his very eyes.

"Born with the talent of ages, building both martial arts and martial legacy simultaneously."

"...Grandfather."

"I showed many shameful sights. I am ashamed. If you return safely, then you will see a changed main family..."

Kuluk.

His words were cut short by blood spilling from his lips.

"Family Head!"

"What is this..."

While the Ma family members rushed forward with stricken faces, Ma Yeon-jeok smiled bitterly at the blood leaking between the fingers covering his mouth.

"This bad already? And I only went to South Zhili once."

He ignored Zhu Lianting and the elders. He only shook his head while reaching toward the startled Jeong Yeon-shin and Ma Se-in.

"It's karma. The unorthodox path I walked in my youth has come back as arrows. I didn't know what moderation meant -- I truly took too many heads. It's only natural for martial arts built up hastily to crumble."

"The reason you can't go to Sichuan..."

Ma Yeon-jeok shook his head at Jeong Yeon-shin's words.

"Law. Because of law. Protecting the main fortress comes first. That alone."

His words carried the stubbornness of the former Divine Sword Group Leader.

A deteriorating body. Declining skill.

The Divine Sword Squads' fighting forces scattered across the chaotic world. Even if both were reasons, he spoke only of law.

Yet as he looked at Jeong Yeon-shin with nothing but pride in his eyes, he soon turned away. His purple robes swirled with the motion.

"I pray for your martial fortune. I always have, and always will."

Ma Yeon-jeok spoke with his back turned.

"Ma family members, follow this old man after seeing our guests out."

The command that followed was not for Jeong Yeon-shin. Ma Yeon-jeok spoke distinctly.

"Zhu Lianting showed behavior beyond reason. The many elders silently cooperated with that conduct. Listening quietly, I did not expect it would go this far. I cannot tolerate today's insubordination. Imperial Golden Immortal Eight Methods? No -- if we truly speak of family revival, I must first establish rules and discipline properly. Even throwing away my face and disregarding life and death, I will punish."

Even his low, measured pronunciation was different. The weight of his years descended heavily.

The purple-rank master, the Desolate Ma Family Head, spoke of punishment. Had it truly ever happened before?

For the first time, bewilderment crept into Zhu Lianting's voice.

"Family Head Elder...!"

"I said follow. How shallow is your martial arts that you dare look down on this seat? Having suffered great shame before my grandson, I must know. I won't draw my sword. I might kill in a single strike."

Those were the final words.

Jeong Yeon-shin left the Desolate Ma family grounds with the Lightning Genius legacy warriors. The atmosphere demanded it.

But a warrior's keen senses clearly detected the energy erupting beyond the threshold.

Kung!

The explosive sound of a foot technique striking an abdomen, the trace of Zhu Lianting's long breath scattering miserably, even Ma Yeon-jeok's dignified coughing.

"My, how savage."

Lazy Flame Dragon, who had stuck close to Jeong Yeon-shin, murmured.

The boy moved in silence. Pushing his worry for Ma Se-in to a corner of his heart.

The presence of warriors following behind him was tremendous.

Enough forces to form a fighting unit overnight had come seeking him.

With this many, the Administration would gladly approve departure for Sichuan. What remained was persuasion.

"I heard about Radiant Demon Wing."

Crimson Day Sword spoke suddenly.

Step.

Jeong Yeon-shin stopped abruptly and turned. Crimson Day Sword continued.

"While tracking the Grandmaster's trail, we also stopped at the Administration. Incidents like this spread quickly through the main fortress."

"..."

"If you need us, say so."

"I need you."

Jeong Yeon-shin answered without hesitation.

His gaze swept over the warriors' faces.

Those who silently nodded or lifted their lips in quiet resolve. Traces of the unique martial arts Jeong Yeon-shin had created were clearly felt in each of them.

They offered words one by one, their tones reverent. They also shared observations among themselves.

"What the Grandmaster should know as well is that Sichuan's murim... can only be described as chaos spread across the central plains."

"Mm. One of the Eight Families and three of the Nine Great Sects, three of the Thirteen Evil Sects. Seven major factions entangled in one region. Can you imagine?"

"Nowhere else in the world compares. With such massive populations of both masters and civilians, talent is as abundant as it is expendable. Martial artists die in droves there. The schemes are equally vast."

"Well, we finished discussing all this on the way here. We agreed it's the perfect place to demonstrate the Lightning Genius legacy martial arts. Besides, we're going to rescue comrades. How could we not follow? The blood runs hot."

At a young white-rank warrior's words, Crimson Day Sword turned to Jeong Yeon-shin.

Moonlight pouring from the vast night sky cast the shadows of the boy and warriors in unison.

"Though Sichuan's murim is vast and unending, our side has warriors bound by a heaven-given teacher."

He raised clasped hands in a deep bow.

"Draw the Desolate Sword at the front. Our complete Grandmaster."
